unit_08 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_08

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 327 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
argumentativecriticismvision
argumentative: standard unit headword | criticism: standard unit headword | vision: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
enterpriseenterprising
enterprise: standard unit headword | enterprising: standard unit headword
#3 Sentence Equivalence
Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments have tried to show.Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments have tried to show.
答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless
arguablyoverly
arguably: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword
#4 SE
It is surprising, given the exquisite of its arguments, that the central thesis of A Theory of Justice can be stated so simply.It is surprising, given the exquisite of its arguments, that the central thesis of A Theory of Justice can be stated so simply.
答案：B, D　nuance; subtlety
argumentthesis
argument: standard unit headword | thesis: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
Alpers and Baxandall remark that Tiepolo is not a recondite painter but rather is quite __________ even to the uninitiated.Alpers and Baxandall remark that Tiepolo is not a recondite painter but rather is quite __________ even to the uninitiated.
答案：B　accessible
prolificimpenetrable
prolific: standard unit headword | impenetrable: standard unit headword
#6 TC DOUBLE
The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i)________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii)________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i)________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii)________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　constrain; vitiate
argueconstrain
argue: standard unit headword | constrain: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
deferencebumbling
deference: standard unit headword | bumbling: standard unit headword

The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.
答案：D　flummery
consequentialcalumny

Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)______ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)______ approach.Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)______ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)______ approach.

Blank 2
答案：C, D　purports; old-fashioned

Blank 2
Blank 3

There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)______. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)______ claims regarding the (ii)______ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)______. Without money, there is no science. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：C, D　indirect; an inference
volatile

答案：A, C　irony; incongruity
avow
avow: standard unit headword

It is difficult to fathom why today's intellectual historians adopt such __________ approach in their work, which seems determined to constrict past thinkers within such narrow social contexts that the wider significance of their ideas disappears.It is difficult to fathom why today's intellectual historians adopt such __________ approach in their work, which seems determined to constrict past thinkers within such narrow social contexts that the wider significance of their ideas disappears.
答案：A　a deflationary
constrict

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. Blank 2
Blank 3

#22 Text Completion
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis

hone: standard unit headword

The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) ____________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) ____________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) ____________ children whose nature is to be inactive.The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) ____________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) ____________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) ____________ children whose nature is to be inactive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　consistent; intrinsic; will have little impact on
affect
affect: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　affords; getting take-out food; former

Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort

#50 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in
chronic
chronic: standard unit headword

#103 TC DOUBLE
Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) _________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) _________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　forgiving of; obfuscation
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#104 TC TRIPLE
Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　prioritize; deficient; ancillary to
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#105 SE
How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of ,though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of ,though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.
答案：C, E　speculation; conjecture
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#106 Sentence Equivalence
To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a ______ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a ______ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.
答案：C, E　paltry; meager
criticismpaltry
criticism: standard unit headword | paltry: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
While some mathematicians regard word problems as a useful pedagogical tool, others argue that they tend to be so __________ that, paradoxically, they give the impression that mathematics has no relevance to the real world.While some mathematicians regard word problems as a useful pedagogical tool, others argue that they tend to be so __________ that, paradoxically, they give the impression that mathematics has no relevance to the real world.
答案：B　contrived
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#108 TC DOUBLE
When the columnist wrote about controversial issues, her own position was not always (i) __________ her analysis of the arguments being put forth on either side. If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.When the columnist wrote about controversial issues, her own position was not always (i) __________ her analysis of the arguments being put forth on either side. If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inferable from; challenged
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#109 Text Completion
The prosecutor belied his hard-boiled reputation by submitting (i) ______ queries to the witness and accepting in turn (ii) ______ responses.The prosecutor belied his hard-boiled reputation by submitting (i) ______ queries to the witness and accepting in turn (ii) ______ responses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　innocuous; evasive
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
criticism
criticism: standard unit headword
#111 SE
The historian argued that __________ are essential to science, claiming that these inherited and untested beliefs often form the conceptual framework necessary for further research.The historian argued that __________ are essential to science, claiming that these inherited and untested beliefs often form the conceptual framework necessary for further research.
答案：C, F　preconceptions; prejudices
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#112 Text Completion
Recent years have seen a disheartening string of revelations in which everyday items once considered (i)______ are found to contain (ii)______ chemicals.Recent years have seen a disheartening string of revelations in which everyday items once considered (i)______ are found to contain (ii)______ chemicals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　innocuous; baneful
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword

The argument, while important, combines analysis and __________, swinging in tone from patient consideration to bitter invective.The argument, while important, combines analysis and __________, swinging in tone from patient consideration to bitter invective.
答案：E　polemic
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#114 TC DOUBLE
Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　wrangling; frisson
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#115 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#116 Text Completion
The author of this travel guide (i)______ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)______, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.The author of this travel guide (i)______ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)______, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　purports; erratic

#117 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolute; qualified

#118 SE

答案：C, F　renounce; disavow

#119 Text Completion
In the 1980s, many historians sounded urgent calls for (i)______ in American historical writing, as longer and longer monographs on smaller and smaller subjects were being written—dazzling studies, but pieces of a puzzle no one was putting together. This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)______ them.In the 1980s, many historians sounded urgent calls for (i)______ in American historical writing, as longer and longer monographs on smaller and smaller subjects were being written—dazzling studies, but pieces of a puzzle no one was putting together. This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)______ them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　synthesis; obscuring
synthesis
synthesis: standard unit headword

The character whose fate dominates the film's plot appears only briefly onscreen, a bit of narrative slyness that we might welcome as __________ the sensory overload more characteristic of this director.The character whose fate dominates the film's plot appears only briefly onscreen, a bit of narrative slyness that we might welcome as __________ the sensory overload more characteristic of this director.
答案：E　a palliative to
sensory
sensory: standard unit headword
#121 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
enterprise
enterprise: standard unit headword
#122 Text Completion
The chairman, faced with the need to forge a consensus on a number of proposals, acknowledged that it would be difficult to reconcile the push for a radical overhaul with the stance of those who want ______ change.The chairman, faced with the need to forge a consensus on a number of proposals, acknowledged that it would be difficult to reconcile the push for a radical overhaul with the stance of those who want ______ change.
答案：A　deliberate
deliberate
deliberate: standard unit headword
#123 TC SINGLE
Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C　prudent

argue: standard unit headword
#124 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　on the periphery of; outsider

#125 SE
The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.
答案：D, E　truculence; pugnacity

argue: standard unit headword
#126 Text Completion
Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)______. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)______. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)______. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)______. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal
atypical
atypical: standard unit headword
#127 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
underscore
underscore: standard unit headword
#128 Sentence Equivalence
Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments to medical researchers have tried to show.Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are ______, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments to medical researchers have tried to show.
答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless
arguablyoverly
arguably: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword

Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)______ that other uses of the act can (ii)______ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)______.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)______ that other uses of the act can (ii)______ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
duplicate
duplicate: standard unit headword

答案：B　sacrificing rigor

They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious

Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved the souvenirs the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved the souvenirs the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere need
decry

Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social

答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed

In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to

#141 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror

#152 Text Completion
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces

Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against

答案：C, D　novel; original

The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.

答案：E　misleading

答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

Blank 2

答案：B, D　uncertain; tenuous

答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught

答案：D, F　paucity; dearth

答案：E, F　tempered; modulated

答案：C, D　respite; relief

答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous

overstate: standard unit headword
#214 Sentence Equivalence
Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually ______ the gathering of important data.Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually ______ the gathering of important data.
答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate

答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity
rectitude
rectitude: standard unit headword
#216 Text Completion
Argument may be an overly (i)______ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Humming birds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii)______ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.Argument may be an overly (i)______ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Humming birds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii)______ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　robust; emulation

答案：A, C　diffuse; spread

In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.

答案：D, F　revival; renaissance

Blank 2

答案：B, E　galvanize; animate

affect: standard unit headword

Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.

The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.

Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

答案：D　disingenuous

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity

#264 Text Completion
The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　self-aggrandizing; value; a product
arguably
arguably: standard unit headword
#265 Text Completion
The company suffers from an almost total lack of ______: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.The company suffers from an almost total lack of ______: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.
答案：C　comity

答案：C, F, H　appealing; useless; highly valuable

Blank 1

答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable

#312 Text Completion
Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)______ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)______. Thus today in the (iii)______ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)______ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)______. Thus today in the (iii)______ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence

#313 Text Completion
When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii)______ rather than—as it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii)______ rather than—as it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code

#321 Text Completion
To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)______ the United States, but his (ii)______ was stemmed not from his (iii)______ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)______ the United States, but his (ii)______ was stemmed not from his (iii)______ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace
